mcqs.question: Consider the relation Student(SID, SName, Age, Dept). Which relational algebra expression retrieves names of all students aged 20?
mcqs.explanation: Projection (π) is applied after selection (σ) to retrieve only SName; applying projection first would lose the Age attribute needed for the selection condition.

mcqs.question: Given relations R(A,B) and S(A,C), the result of R ⋈ S (natural join) will contain which attributes?

options.text: A, B, C, D (if D exists)

mcqs.explanation: Natural join combines tuples with equal values on common attributes (A) and includes all non-common attributes (B and C) from both relations.

mcqs.question: Using the schema Enroll(SID, CID, Grade) and Course(CID, CName, Credits), identify the relational algebra expression to find course names enrolled by student SID='S101'.
mcqs.explanation: First restrict Enroll to student S101, then natural join with Course to get matching CID, then project CName.

mcqs.question: The division operator in relational algebra is used to find tuples in relation R that are related to ALL tuples in relation S. Given R(A,B) and S(B), what does R ÷ S return?

options.text: All A values that are associated with every B value in S
All B values that are associated with every A value in R
Cartesian product of R and S

mcqs.explanation: Division returns attribute A from R for which every B in S has a matching tuple in R, essentially 'R contains S'.

mcqs.question: Consider two relations: R(A,B) = {(a1,b1), (a1,b2), (a2,b1)} and S(B) = {(b1), (b2)}. What is the result of R ∩ S?
mcqs.explanation: Set operations (∪, ∩, −) require union-compatible schemas; R has 2 attributes while S has 1, so intersection is not defined.

mcqs.question: Which SQL query corresponds to the relational algebra expression: π_SName(σ_Dept='Physics'(Student))?

options.text: SELECT SName FROM Student WHERE Dept = 'Physics';
SELECT SName FROM Student ORDER BY Dept;

mcqs.explanation: σ_Dept='Physics' corresponds to WHERE Dept='Physics', and π_SName corresponds to SELECT SName.

mcqs.question: Given relations: Works(Person, Company, Salary) and Lives(Person, City). To find persons who work in 'TCS' and live in 'Delhi', the correct expression is:
mcqs.explanation: Finding persons who satisfy BOTH conditions requires intersection of two projections: persons working in TCS AND persons living in Delhi.

mcqs.question: Using Enroll(SID, CID) and Course(CID, CName, Credits), find course IDs that are enrolled by ALL students (i.e., every student takes that course). The correct relational algebra expression is:
mcqs.explanation: Division finds CID values from Enroll that are related to every SID in Enroll; using the same relation ensures we find courses taken by ALL students who have enrolled in any course.
